blocks.text: Re: Integration Support for Reeves, Miller and Thompson
Dear Mr. Cole:

This letter confirms the terms on which Pinebrook Advisory Group will support {{fact:f:E-2021-001.client}} through the integration of the business you have acquired. You put the task plainly when we met: the deal is closed, the two organizations are still run as two, and the window in which an acquisition either comes together or quietly drifts apart is the one you are in now. Our work is to give that integration a plan, a pace, and an honest account of where it stands, so the value you paid for does not leak away in the months after the signing. What follows sets out the scope, the team, the fee, and the standard terms that will govern our work together. If anything here needs adjusting before we begin, tell me and we will settle it.
Scope and Approach

The engagement runs for approximately seven months and moves in three overlapping phases. First, we will take stock of both organizations as they actually operate today, function by function, so that the plan rests on how the work gets done rather than on the org chart. Second, we will build a single integration plan with you: the sequence of moves across leadership and reporting lines, finance and systems, operations, commercial continuity, and people, each with a named owner and a plain measure of done. Third, we will help your team run that plan, holding a regular cadence that surfaces what is on track, what is stuck, and what needs a decision, and keeping the whole effort visible on one page rather than scattered across a dozen.

Two principles shape how we work. The decisions and the running of the business stay with your people; we bring the structure, the analysis, and the discipline to keep the integration moving, but we do not sign for you. And we will tell you what we see, including the parts that are behind or harder than hoped, because an integration status that only ever reads green is the surest way to be surprised late. Matters outside the integration itself, such as the tax and legal treatment of the transaction, are not part of this engagement; where we happen to notice something in those areas we will flag it for you rather than opine on it.
Engagement Team

I will oversee the engagement personally, as I do for all of our work, and stay close enough to it to answer for the result. Jennifer Fletcher, a senior consultant with our firm, will lead the integration day to day and be your principal point of contact; she is at her best when a situation is delicate and the people questions are real. Robert Miller, a principal consultant, will carry the operational analysis, tracing how each function actually runs so the plan reflects the floor and not the theory. Cynthia Ball, our analyst, will build and keep the integration tracker and the exhibits that let all of us read the same picture at a glance. On your side, we understand you will sponsor the work and clear the path to your people and records, which is the single thing that most determines how well an integration like this goes.
Fee and Timing

Our fee for this engagement is a fixed professional fee of {{fact:f:E-2021-001.fee}}, covering all of the work described above. We expect to begin on {{fact:f:E-2021-001.start}} and to carry the engagement through approximately seven months of integration work. The fixed fee is not tied to hours; it covers the engagement as scoped, so you know your cost at the outset and it does not move with the effort we put in. If, as the work proceeds, we and you agree that the scope should change in a way that materially affects the fee, we will put that in writing and settle it with you before doing the additional work.
Fees and Payment

The fixed professional fee of {{fact:f:E-2021-001.fee}} will be invoiced in equal monthly installments over the term of the engagement, and each invoice is payable within thirty days of receipt. Reasonable out-of-pocket expenses, if any, will be billed at cost and itemized, and we will clear any expense of consequence with you before we incur it.
Term and Termination

This engagement runs for the term of the integration work, approximately seven months. Either of us may end it earlier on written notice. If that happens, you will owe fees for the work performed through the date of termination, and we will hand over the integration plan, the tracker, and our working analysis in usable form so your team can carry it forward without us.
Confidentiality

An integration puts us close to sensitive ground: the acquisition terms, your people decisions, and the plans of both organizations. We will treat everything we learn as confidential and use it only for this engagement, and our people are bound by the same obligation. In turn, we ask that the plans and analysis we prepare for you stay within your organization, since they are built for your circumstances rather than for general reliance. Either of us may of course disclose what the law or a regulator requires.
Limitation of Liability

Our aggregate liability to Reeves, Miller and Thompson for any claim arising out of this engagement will be limited to the fees paid to us for the work, and we do not accept liability for indirect or consequential losses. This is not a matter of confidence in the work; it reflects the advisory nature of what we do. The plan and the analysis are ours to make well, but the integration decisions, and their execution, remain yours.
Governing Law

This engagement will be governed by the laws of the state in which our firm maintains its principal office, without regard to its conflict-of-laws rules. Any dispute we cannot resolve between us will be handled in the courts of that state.

If these terms are acceptable, please sign below and return one copy to us, keeping the other for your files. We are glad to be doing this work with you, and we will be ready to begin on the date set out above. Thank you for your confidence in the firm.
Sincerely,
